Gene_Accession_Number.reason: Experimental procedures explicitly give QsuB from C. glutamicum with GenBank accession number YP_001137362.1.

Enzyme_Name.reason: Title, Summary, Results, and Figure 1 identify QsuB as a 3-dehydroshikimate dehydratase.

EC_Number.reason: No EC number is stated anywhere in the provided paper text.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: QsuB is explicitly described as a bacterial 3-dehydroshikimate dehydratase; while no domain analysis is given, this family-level description is stated.

Source_Species.reason: QsuB is explicitly said to be from Corynebacterium glutamicum.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The full Latin name Corynebacterium glutamicum is explicitly provided.

Applied_Species.reason: Results and methods repeatedly state expression in Arabidopsis.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: QsuB was fused to a plastid-targeting peptide and transient expression in tobacco confirmed plastid localization.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper explicitly places 3-dehydroshikimate in the shikimate pathway and shows that QsuB expression primarily affects lignin/phenylpropanoid biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: This exact phrase is not used. It is inferable that QsuB diverts a shikimate intermediate affecting lignin precursor supply, but the named 'interface' is author interpretation, not paper wording.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The paper shows QsuB creates a diversion from 3-dehydroshikimate to protocatechuate, but it does not explicitly classify this as a 'side-route'.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Summary and Figure 1 explicitly state QsuB converts 3-dehydroshikimate.

Primary_Product.reason: Summary and Figure 1 explicitly state conversion to protocatechuate.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results state QsuB was targeted to plastids to convert 3-dehydroshikimate into protocatechuate.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Results and discussion support that QsuB indirectly alters monolignol precursor pools and lignin composition/content rather than acting directly in monolignol synthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The core measured end product affected is lignin.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Lignin is indeed a phenylpropanoid polymer, but this exact classification phrase is not stated in the paper text.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: Lignin deposition/content was measured in stems/cell walls, and QsuB was expressed in lignifying tissues.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: Summary explicitly states lignin confers recalcitrance; cell wall rigidity is also consistent with the introduction's description of lignin deposition in cell walls.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: All listed effects are supported by Results: reduced lignin content, increased H units, reduced G units, lower polymerization degree, improved saccharification, and increased protocatechuate.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper does not establish QsuB as rate-limiting; presenting it as 'not established' and as an engineered indirect flux-diverting step matches the evidence.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: Methods report 22°C, 10 h light, 60% humidity growth conditions and saccharification after hot water, dilute alkali, or dilute acid pretreatments.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: Results state the C4H promoter was used for expression in lignifying tissues and Western blot confirmed expression in stems.

Applied_Promoters.reason: The Arabidopsis C4H promoter is explicitly described and used.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods appear in Results/methods: transgenic Arabidopsis, tobacco YFP localization, Western blot, LC-MS, Klason lignin assay, pyro-GC/MS, HSQC NMR, SEC, and saccharification assays.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each numbered point matches explicit Results: 67-113-fold protocatechuate increase, precursor shifts, 45-52% lignin reduction with H increase/G decrease, lower polymerization and >2-fold saccharification under some conditions, and reduced height with slight biomass penalty in one line.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: Discussion explicitly calls QsuB a promising gain-of-function strategy for bioenergy crops and suggests combining it with more stringent/fibre-specific promoters to reduce defects.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Results and discussion report reduced plant height in all lines, slight biomass decrease in one line, and evidence of vessel collapse in a strongly expressing line.

Other_Important_Info.reason: Discussion explicitly states QsuB did not affect overall aromatic amino acid/salicylate levels and discusses possible indirect effects via shikimate availability or protocatechuate competition with HCT.

corrections.reason: No EC number is stated anywhere in the provided paper text.
